title: helleborine latifolia f. gracilis
intro: Helleborine latifolia f. gracilis, also known as the lesser helleborine or lady's lantern, is a perennial orchid native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Helleborine latifolia f. gracilis include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant are typically harvested and used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
